title: Expressing Opinions
situation: You just watched a Bollywood movie with friends and everyone's sharing their take on it.

phrases.context: 'mujhe lagta/lagi' = I feel/felt — your go-to opinion structure

phrases.hindi: yaar, first half boring tha na?
phrases.english: Dude, the first half was boring right?

phrases.hindi: haan but end mein twist tha, woh mast tha
phrases.english: Yeah but there was a twist at the end, that was cool

phrases.hindi: mujhe nahi lagta ki itni acchi thi
phrases.english: I don't think it was that good
phrases.context: Polite disagreement — 'mujhe nahi lagta' = I don't feel/think

phrases.hindi: acting toh kamaal thi lekin story weak thi
phrases.english: The acting was amazing though but story was weak

grammar_notes: Past tense uses 'tha/thi/the': masculine singular = tha, feminine singular = thi, plural = the
'Mujhe lagta hai' = I think/feel (present). 'Mujhe laga' = I felt (past)
'toh' adds emphasis to whatever comes before it — 'woh toh accha tha' = that WAS good
culture_notes: Indians are very expressive about movies — strong opinions are expected and fun
Using English words mixed in ('boring', 'twist', 'acting') is totally normal in Hindi conversation

skill_breakdown.explanation: Simple past: was/were. Masculine = tha, Feminine = thi, Plural/Respect = the. Matches the SUBJECT not the speaker. 'Film acchi thi' (film=feminine, so thi).

more_examples.english: who all were at the party?

skill_breakdown.explanation: 'Lagta hai' = it seems/I think. Most natural way to express opinions. Structure: mujhe + [adjective] + lagta/lagti + hai. Past: mujhe + [adjective] + laga/lagi.

more_examples.hindi: mujhe lagta hai woh sahi bol raha hai

more_examples.english: how did you find it? (what did you feel?)

skill_breakdown.explanation: Agree: 'haan bilkul' (yes absolutely), 'sahi baat hai' (that's right), 'ekdum' (exactly). Disagree politely: 'mujhe nahi lagta' (I don't think so), 'haan lekin...' (yeah but...), 'ho sakta hai par...' (could be but...).

more_examples.english: no dude, it's not like that

more_examples.hindi: ho sakta hai, par mujhe alag laga
more_examples.english: could be, but I felt differently

practice_prompt: You and a friend just watched 'Animal' on Netflix. You thought it was too long but the acting was great. Your friend loved everything about it. Discuss the movie — share your opinion, agree on some things, politely disagree on others.
